issue.title: Crash on empty config file
issue.body:
## Steps to reproduce
1. Create an empty `config.yaml`
2. Run `widget start`

## Expected
It should start with defaults.

## Actual
It throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'port')` at `src/config.ts:42`.

Environment: widget v2.1.0, Node 20. This reproduces 100% of the time.

issue.title: It doesn't work
issue.body: I tried to use the thing and it didn't work. Please fix.

issue.title: Add a --json output flag to `widget status`
issue.body:
## Use case
We script around `widget status` in CI and parsing the human table is brittle.

## Proposal
Add a `--json` flag that prints the same status as a JSON object: `{ "state": "running", "uptimeSec": 1234 }`.

The table output stays the default. This is additive and well-scoped — the status data already exists in `src/status.ts`.

problem_statement: How do I change the port?

issue.title: How do I change the listening port?
issue.body: Quick question — what's the right way to change the port widget listens on? Is it an env var or a config key? Not a bug, just want to understand the options.
